answer:
We have that \begin{align*}
\sin{2\alpha} + \sin{2\beta} + \sin{2\gamma} & = 2\sin\left(\frac{2\alpha+2\beta}{2}\right)\cos\left(\frac{2\alpha-2\beta}{2}\right) + 2\sin\left(\frac{2\beta+2\gamma}{2}\right)\cos\left(\frac{2\beta-2\gamma}{2}\right)\\
& = 2\sin(\alpha+\beta)\cos(\alpha-\beta) + 2\sin(\beta+\gamma)\cos(\beta-\gamma)\\
& = 2\sin(\alpha+\beta)\cos(\alpha-\beta) + 2\sin(180^{\circ}-\alpha-\gamma)\cos(180^{\circ}-(\alpha+\beta))\\
& = 2\sin(\alpha+\beta)\cos(\alpha-\beta) - 2\sin(\alpha+\beta)\cos(\alpha+\beta)\\
& = -2\sin(\alpha+\beta)\left(\cos(\alpha-\beta) - \cos(\alpha+\beta)\right)\\
& = -2\sin(\alpha+\beta)\left(2\sin\left(\frac{\alpha+\beta-\alpha+\beta}{2}\right)\cos\left(\frac{\alpha+\beta+\alpha-\beta}{2}\right)\right)\\
& = -4\sin(\alpha+\beta)\sin(\beta)\cos(\alpha)\\
& = -4\sin(\alpha)\sin(\beta)\cos(\alpha)\\
& = -2\sin(2\alpha)\sin(\beta)\\
& \leq 2|\sin(2\alpha)||\sin(\beta)|\\
& \leq 2\cdot 1\cdot 1\\
& = 2
\end{align*}where the last inequality follows from the fact that $|\sin(\theta)|\leq 1$ for all $\theta$.

The maximum value is attained when $\alpha = 45^{\circ}$ and $\beta = 67.5^{\circ}$.

\boxed{2}
